interpretation_summary: The replication followed the preregistered plan to test whether severe psychological distress predicts lower motivation for distance learning using an ordered logistic regression on a Prolific-based sample. After minor code fixes (entrypoint execution and CI computation for the MNLogit robustness), the analysis ran successfully (analytic N=733). The focal result (Severe vs No Distress) yielded OR=0.331 (95% CI [0.228, 0.481], p≈6.7e-09), confirming a strong inverse association, similar in direction (and somewhat stronger in magnitude) to the original finding (OR=0.45, 95% CI [0.28, 0.70], p<0.0001). Overall, the replication supports the focal claim.

fidelity_assessment.method_alignment: Full alignment with preregistration: The code applied the specified timestamp filter and targeted Kessler imputations, constructed K10 distress categories, and fit an ordered logistic regression of online_learning_1 on distress categories (No Distress as baseline). The planned MNLogit robustness specification was also executed. No additional covariates were included, matching the focal test.

deviations.issue_description: Entrypoint script initially imported analysis modules without executing them; modified run_replication__py.py to call analysis functions explicitly.
deviations.impact: Low: Procedural fix; did not change analysis specification or results.

deviations.issue_description: MNLogit confidence interval extraction failed due to conf_int indexing; replaced with manual CI computation from params and bse.
deviations.impact: Low: Affects robustness output formatting only; focal result unaffected.

deviations.issue_description: Date parsing produced warnings due to unspecified format/timezone for the created timestamp filter.
deviations.impact: Low: The intended cutoff was applied consistently; no evidence of material effect on N or results.

results_comparison.hypothesis_tested: Students with higher psychological distress (Severe Distress on K10) will have lower odds of higher motivation for distance learning (online_learning_1), with no covariates.
results_comparison.original_results: Inverse association reported: Severe distress associated with lower motivation for distance learning (OR=0.45, 95% CI [0.28, 0.70], p<0.0001; N≈381).
results_comparison.replication_results: Ordered logistic regression on replication data (analytic N=733): Severe vs No Distress OR=0.331, 95% CI [0.228, 0.481], p=6.73e-09; direction negative and statistically significant. ORs for Low and Moderate distress were also <1 and significant.
results_comparison.overall_answer: Yes. The replication meets the preregistered criteria (OR<1, 95% CI excluding 1) for the focal claim; direction and significance are consistent with the original.

replication_report: Replication successful: Severe psychological distress is associated with substantially lower odds of higher motivation for distance learning (OR≈0.33, p<0.001), consistent with the original report (OR≈0.45), albeit with a somewhat stronger effect in the replication sample.

failure_handling.suggestions: Ensure entrypoint scripts explicitly call analysis functions; for statsmodels MNLogit CIs, compute from params and bse or use get_margeff for interpretable effects if conf_int indexing is unstable.
Specify explicit datetime formats/timezones for robust filtering; validate Kessler item completeness and document any imputations applied.

notes: Replication used an independent Prolific-based sample with different country/context and later collection period vs. the original Jordanian student sample; effect sizes may differ due to sample/composition changes. The ordered logit assumes proportional odds (not tested here); future work could assess this and add covariate adjustments for robustness. Despite these caveats, the focal inverse association replicated strongly in direction and significance.
